tasks.title: Surface artifact deletion failures in retention batch deletes
evidence: path: internal/store/store_retention.go :: DeleteJobsWithArtifactsBatch :: failedDeletions is incremented but never returned or logged, so artifact removal failures are silently ignored
path: internal/store/store_retention.go :: DeleteJobsWithArtifactsBatch :: spaceReclaimedMB is computed before deletes and returned even if deleteJobArtifacts fails
plan: Decide how to surface artifact deletion failures (return aggregated error, include failed IDs in result, or attach to CleanupResult.Errors)
Adjust DeleteJobsWithArtifactsBatch to report failures and keep spaceReclaimedMB consistent with actual deletions
Update retention engine handling to propagate/report partial deletion errors
Add store/retention tests that simulate artifact deletion failure and assert error reporting + spaceReclaimedMB behavior
Verify by running `go test ./internal/store -run DeleteJobsWithArtifactsBatch` and confirming failures are reported

tasks.title: Fix retention cleanup skipping matching kinds when first batch mismatches
evidence: path: internal/retention/retention.go :: cleanupByAge :: when opts.Kind filters out all jobs in a batch, len(toDelete)==0 triggers break, so later matching jobs are never considered
path: internal/retention/retention.go :: cleanupByCount/cleanupByStorage :: same len(toDelete)==0 break causes early exit when first batch contains only other kinds
plan: Adjust cleanupByAge/cleanupByCount/cleanupByStorage loops to continue scanning when the current batch has no matching kinds (avoid premature break)
Add or extend retention tests to cover the scenario where early batches are non-matching kinds but later batches match
Ensure loop termination still occurs when no more jobs are available to avoid infinite loops
Verify by running `go test ./internal/retention -run Cleanup` and confirming kind-filtered cleanup deletes the expected jobs

tasks.title: Treat missing jobs directory as zero storage in GetStorageStats
evidence: path: internal/store/store_retention.go :: getTotalStorageSizeMB :: filepath.WalkDir is called on dataDir/jobs without handling the root-not-exist error, so missing jobs dir returns an error instead of zero storage
plan: Handle a missing jobs directory by returning 0 bytes before walking or by translating os.IsNotExist errors from WalkDir
Add a store retention test that calls GetStorageStats with no jobs directory and asserts it returns zero storage without error
Verify by running `go test ./internal/store -run GetStorageStats` and confirming a clean pass
